fullArticle:
All products featured here are independently selected by our editors and writers. If you buy something through links on our site, Gizmodo may earn an affiliate commission.

In-ear monitors can be a daunting category of personal audio to get into. Not only is there a whole new lexicon you’ll have to learn before pulling the trigger, but the cost can be steep, setting you back several hundred dollars, if not thousands. They can be daunting, but they don’t have to be.

Sony’s new in-ear monitors, the IER-M500, just debuted at $120, marking a reasonable starting price for anyone looking to dip their toes. The wired earbuds come with a 5mm dynamic driver and have a frequency response between 10 Hz and 40,000 Hz, which should make them capable of relaying deep bass and high treble. Part of the r...
